sections.paragraph:
πλήρωμα, ατος, τό [πληρόω]

—1. ‘that which is there as result of filling’

—a. content 1 Cor 10:26; ἦραν ϰλάσματα δώδεκα ϰοφίνων πληρώματα they took up frag-ments amounting to twelve basketfuls Mk 6:43; sim. 8:20; fullness Ro 15:29; Eph 3:19.

—b. w. focus on the product patch Mt 9:16; Mk 2:21; fullness, the ἐκκλησία as Christ’s body fully supplied Eph 1:23.

—2. ‘peak amount’, fullness Ro 11:12, 25.

—3. ‘that which is characteristic to a maximum degree’, fullness

—a. of God Col 1:19; 2:9.

—b. of Christ J 1:16; Eph 4:13.

—4. ‘stage in time marking the attainment of a designed objective’, fullness Gal 4:4; Eph 1:10.

—5. ‘completion of a requirement’, fulfillment Ro 13:10.
